intro: Rytidosperma pictum, also known as kangaroo grass, is a species of perennial grass native to Australia, characterized by its distinctive yellow or white inflorescences. The plant has been traditionally used by indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rytidosperma pictum include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, kangaroo grass is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin irritations.
